zh.Church of Saint John Baptist, Pitney, So: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特皮特尼
zh.Pitney, South Somerset, Somerset, Englan: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特皮特尼
zh.lifeSummary: 當Marjorie Walls Allen於1151年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特皮特尼時，她的父親Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall28歲，母親Baroness Jane Wogan25歲。她於1174年與Richard Reynell I在英國英格蘭薩默塞特皮特尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1203年在她的家鄉享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特皮特尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marjorie Walls Allen于1151年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特皮特尼时，她的父亲Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall28岁，母亲Baroness Jane Wogan25岁。她于1174年与Richard Reynell I在英国英格兰薩默塞特皮特尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1203年在她的家乡享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特皮特尼。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Marjorie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual modern spelling of Margery . It seems to have arisen as the result of folk etymological association of the name with that of the herb marjoram ( compare Rosemary ). This word is of uncertain origin; its Middle English and Old French form was majorane, without the first -r-.

variants.name: Marjory
meanings: The usual modern spelling of Margery . It seems to have arisen as the result of folk etymological association of the name with that of the herb marjoram ( compare Rosemary ). This word is of uncertain origin; its Middle English and Old French form was majorane, without the first -r-.

nameMeanings.name: Walls
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Walter   or, less commonly, of   Wallace .  It has dropped almost completely out of fashion, especially since the advent in the 20th century of the slang term, denoting a stupid or incompetent person.

en.lifeSummary: When Marjorie Walls Allen was born in 1151, in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall, was 28 and her mother, Baroness Jane Wogan, was 25. She married Richard Reynell I in 1174, in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1203, in her hometown, at the age of 52, and was buried in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marjorie Walls Allen est née en 1151, à Pitney, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Baroness Jane Wogan, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Reynell I en 1174, à Pitney, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1203, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Pitney, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marjorie Walls Allen 1151 in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall 28 und ihre Mutter Baroness Jane Wogan 25. Sie hat 1174 in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom Richard Reynell I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1203 im Alter von 52 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Pitney, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marjorie Walls Allen è nata il nel 1151 nel Pitney, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Baroness Jane Wogan, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Richard Reynell I nel 1174, nel Pitney, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1203, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a Pitney, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Marjorie Walls Allenが1151年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトピトニーで生まれた時，父Baron Richard Thomas Cornwallは28歳，母Baroness Jane Woganは25歳であった。1174年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトピトニーでRichard Reynell Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1203年に故郷で52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marjorie Walls Allen(이)가 1151년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Baroness Jane Wogan(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1174년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트니에서 Richard Reynell I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1203년 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marjorie Walls Allen nasceu em 1151, em Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Jane Wogan, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Reynell I em 1174, em Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1203, em sua cidade natal, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marjorie Walls Allen родилась в 1151 г. в местности питни, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness Jane Wogan, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Reynell I в 1174 г. в местности питни, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1203 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности питни, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marjorie Walls Allen nació en 1151, en Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baron Richard Thomas Cornwall, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Baroness Jane Wogan, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Richard Reynell I en 1174, en Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1203, en su pueblo, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Pitney, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
